mission.title: kgateway: `glooctl check` Unable to cancel request for *exec.roundTripper
mission.description: `glooctl check` Unable to cancel request for *exec.roundTripper. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify kgateway troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kgateway deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway -n kgateway --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kgateway configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kgateway configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway
kubectl get configmap -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kgateway
```
Glooctl check returns intermittent error `Unable to cancel request for *exec.roundTripper`

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. kube apply '...'
2. curl '....'
3. See error

Intermittent issue, hard to track down.

steps.title: Apply the fix for `glooctl check` Unable to cancel request for
steps.description:
Extend the timeout for listing secrets during `glooctl check` to prevent noisy messages when there is latency in the request. This value, `secretClientTimeoutSeconds`, defaults to 30, and can be configured in `~/.gloo/glooctl-config.yaml`.
```yaml
Client: {"version":"1.7.11"}
Server: {"type":"Ingress","kubernetes":{"containers":[{"Tag":"1.7.11","Name":"discovery","Registry":"quay.io/solo-io"},{"Tag":"1.7.11","Name":"gateway","Registry":"quay.io/solo-io"},{"Tag":"1.7.11","Name":"gloo-envoy-wrapper","Registry":"quay.io/solo-io"},{"Tag":"1.7.11","Name":"gloo-envoy-wrapper","Registry":"quay.io/solo-io"},{"Tag":"1.7.11","Name":"gloo","Registry":"quay.io/solo-io"},{"Tag":"1.7.11","Name":"ingress","Registry":"quay.io/solo-io"},{"Tag":"1.7.11","Name":"gloo-envoy-wrapper","Registry":"quay.io/solo-io"},{"Tag":"1.7.11","Name":"gloo-envoy-wrapper",
```

steps.title: Confirm `glooctl check` Unable to cancel request for is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway -n kgateway --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kgateway --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Extend the timeout for listing secrets during `glooctl check` to prevent noisy messages when there is latency in the request. This value, `secretClientTimeoutSeconds`, defaults to 30, and can be configured in `~/.gloo/glooctl-config.yaml`.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kgateway installed or the issue environment reproducible.
